meta.title: Character International Academy — TVET & National Skills Certification in Malaysia
meta.description: A JPK-accredited Malaysian TVET institution helping 4,500+ learners earn SKM/DKM/DLKM certification and 80+ companies build structured workforce development. Founded 2002 · HQ Muar, Johor.
meta.ogDescription: 20+ years of structured, competency-based TVET training across Muar, Johor Bahru, Kluang and Seremban. JPK-accredited · SKM · DKM · DLKM · ADI-Perantisan · HRD Corp claimable.
meta.twitterDescription: JPK-accredited Malaysian TVET · SKM / DKM / DLKM · ADI-Perantisan · HRD Corp claimable training. Since 2002.

about.title: A Malaysian TVET institution built by people who run businesses.
about.p1After: ) is a professional TVET institution and corporate system development consultancy dedicated to national skills certification and structured workforce transformation. Our focus is nurturing highly competent professionals with industry-relevant technical expertise and strong professional values.
about.p2: We bridge industry needs with structured national skills frameworks. Through active industry engagement, we've built strategic partnerships with companies across multiple sectors to deliver industry-driven training programmes and apprenticeship opportunities that match real workforce demand — not marketing brochures.
about.p3: Our founder saw the same pattern in every business she ran: organisations leaning on the memory and instinct of long-serving staff instead of on documented, teachable systems. Character Academy was set up to fix that — to bring competency-based training into Malaysian workplaces and tie it to a nationally recognised certificate, so that skilled work becomes a career, not a guess.

vision.title: Where we're going, and what we do each week to get there.
vision.visionTitle: Become a leading force in Malaysia's TVET ecosystem — one where industry-driven education and structured skills development empower sustainable workforce growth.
vision.visionBody: We pursue this through continuous industry collaboration, competency-based training, and nationally recognised certification pathways.
missionPoints: Provide structured, competency-based training aligned with industry needs.
Support workforce development through systematic skills pathways.
Promote national skills certification (SKM · DKM · DLKM).
Develop competent trainers, assessors, and industry practitioners.
Strengthen collaboration between industry and training institutions.

how.title: A six-step backbone. Every engagement. No shortcuts.
how.lead: Needs → Standards → Programme → Implementation → Certification → Sustainability. Skipping a step is how training ends up not fitting the job.

steps.title: Industry & Organisational Needs Analysis
steps.body: We start on the ground. What does the industry actually demand? What's broken inside this specific organisation — a capability gap, a retention problem, an inconsistent training system? The goal is to match what we build next to real operational needs, not to a brochure.

steps.title: Standards Alignment
steps.body: We map those needs to the National Occupational Skills Standard (NOSS) and the national certification framework (SKM · DKM · DLKM). This turns a custom training plan into a nationally recognised one.

steps.title: Programme Design
steps.body: We design competency-based training pathways — modular, level-appropriate, assessable — so that every learner can demonstrate what they can do, not just what they were taught.

steps.title: Implementation
steps.body: We deliver. Classroom learning is integrated with workplace practice — and, where it fits, with the ADI-Perantisan apprenticeship model (70–80% of the training at the employer's site).

steps.title: Certification
steps.body: We facilitate the formal competency assessment. Successful learners leave with an SKM, DKM, or DLKM issued by JPK under the Ministry of Human Resources.

steps.title: Continuous Improvement
steps.body: We stay with the organisation after the certificate is issued — refining the training system, updating matrices, tracking retention, planning the next certification wave. Skills development is a system, not an event.

services.title: Two kinds of people come to us. We built a service line for each.
services.corporateIntro: Structured advisory and implementation services to build sustainable workforce development systems.

corporate.title: TVET Skills Training Programmes
corporate.body: Custom competency-based programmes mapped to NOSS and the SKM/DKM/DLKM framework.

corporate.title: ADI-Perantisan Apprenticeship
corporate.body: End-to-end setup of apprenticeship programmes under the National Dual Training System.

corporate.title: Accredited Training Centre Advisory
corporate.body: Achieve and maintain Pusat Bertauliah accreditation with JPK.

corporate.title: Workforce Development Programmes
corporate.body: Organisation-wide skills roadmaps, competency matrices, and certification pipelines.

corporate.title: Competency-Based Implementation
corporate.body: Hands-on advisory during rollout — not just a plan on paper.

corporate.title: Talent Retention Planning
corporate.body: Link certification and career progression directly to retention outcomes.

corporate.title: Corporate Growth Advisory
corporate.body: Use talent strategy as a lever for business growth, not an HR afterthought.

corporate.title: HRD Corp Claimable Training
corporate.body: Training that employers can claim back under the HRD Corp levy scheme.

services.individualIntro: Pathways for professionals to build recognised credentials and move their career forward.

individual.title: Upskilling Advisory
individual.body: Diagnose the skill gap and map the fastest credible path forward.

individual.title: Certification Pathways Guidance
individual.body: Pick the right SKM / DKM / DLKM level and the most practical route — training, ADI, or PPT (prior learning).

individual.title: Competency Enhancement Guidance
individual.body: One-on-one advisory while you work toward assessment.

individual.title: JPK Accreditation Advisory
individual.body: Guidance for professionals who want to become assessors, verifiers (PPL), or industry experts.

individual.title: HRD Corp Trainer Accreditation
individual.body: Pass the 5-day TTT programme and the 120-hour accreditation process.

projects.title: Three initiatives that show how the approach holds up in practice.

items.title: Industry-Based Apprenticeship Development
items.body: We partnered with more than eighty employers to stand up industry-based apprenticeship programmes aligned with Malaysia's national TVET agenda. Workplace learning was woven together with competency-based classroom training, so apprentices developed skill and context at the same time.

items.title: National Skills Certification Pathway
items.body: Character Academy's largest and longest-running initiative: helping experienced workers obtain formal recognition of what they already know how to do. Through structured pathways — including Recognition of Prior Achievement (PPT) — workers across multiple sectors earned nationally recognised SKM / DKM / DLKM certificates.
outcomes: Credentials that match existing on-the-job competence

items.title: Corporate Workforce Development & Talent Retention
items.body: We helped companies design and implement full talent retention systems — not just training, but the connective tissue around it: competency mapping, training-matrix development, and certification-based career-progression planning.
outcomes: Higher employee capability across the board
Greater training consistency between teams and branches

leadership.title: Two decades of JPK work, led by an industry practitioner.

jennifer.name: Jennifer Tan
jennifer.bio: Founder of Character International Academy. Thirty years in corporate management and two decades running JPK-accredited training work — with a track record that the Department of Skills Development has repeatedly drawn on as a public voice for Malaysian TVET.

timeline.role: as CEO & COO of Character Education Group of Academy
as Managing Director, True Harmony Group of Chain Company

accredited by JPK as Panel of Judges (Panel Hakim)

others.name: SeaQin Teo
others.body: Guides and empowers the academy's training and advisory teams. Day-to-day leadership, cross-team collaboration, performance management, and the continuous improvement loop that keeps our programmes aligned with JPK standards and client outcomes.

others.name: May Liau
others.body: Oversees operations and performance across our four branches. Keeps training quality, operational standards, and service delivery consistent from Muar HQ through Johor Bahru, Kluang and Seremban — and keeps every branch aligned with competency-based training and national certification standards.

testimonials.title: Full testimonials, as written. Trimmed for nothing.

corporate.name: Mr. Tan
corporate.role: CEO, Cottonrose Group · Founder, Supreme Automotive Academy Sdn Bhd

plan.title: Three plans. One direction.
plan.lead: Make structured skills development more accessible, more specialised, and more internationally connected.

items.title: Digital TVET & Hybrid Learning Ecosystem
items.body: A hybrid learning layer that combines physical workshops and on-site training with structured online support. Online handles pre-work, reference material, progress tracking, and remote coaching; physical handles hands-on practice and assessment.
bullets: Scale — regional reach without sacrificing assessment integrity
Accessibility — working professionals progress without stepping away from their jobs
Continuity — competency development does not stop when the classroom empties

items.title: Regional Centres of Excellence
items.body: Specialised Centres of Excellence focused on key industry sectors — acting as hubs for advanced skills development (DKM Level 4, DLKM Level 5, specialist tracks), trainer capacity-building, and sector-level industry collaboration.
bullets: Hubs for advanced DKM / DLKM skills development
Trainer capacity-building for HRD Corp accredited trainers and JPK assessors
Sector-level industry collaboration, not just company-level

items.title: International Collaboration & Talent Mobility
items.body: Partnerships with international training institutions and industry organisations to enable knowledge exchange, raise competency standards, and open cross-border opportunities for certified professionals.
bullets: Knowledge exchange and benchmarking against international best practice
Raise Malaysian competency standards toward international levels
Cross-border opportunities tied to the ASEAN Year of Skills agenda

contact.title: Connect with Jennifer directly.
contact.body: Whether you're building a workforce development system from the ground up, moving a team toward SKM / DKM / DLKM certification, or exploring HRD Corp claimable training — a 20-minute phone call usually tells us both whether we're a fit.
